message:
Introduce sharded TaskTable for hot-path scheduler operations

Three interconnected improvements to reduce contention on the global
RuntimeState lock during high-throughput scheduling:

1. ThreeLaneScheduler: add optional sharded TaskTable
   - New `new_with_options_and_task_table()` constructor accepts an
     Arc<ContendedMutex<TaskTable>> for fine-grained locking
   - When present, LocalQueue instances are backed by the task table
     instead of the full RuntimeState, so push/pop/steal avoid the
     global lock entirely on the hot path
   - Cross-cutting operations (task_completed, drain_ready_async_finalizers)
     still use RuntimeState as before

2. LocalQueue: add `push_many()` batch method
   - Acquires the arena + queue lock once for the entire batch instead of
     per-item, reducing lock acquisition overhead when waking multiple
     waiters simultaneously
   - Includes LIFO-order unit test

3. Worker: cleanup and correctness
   - Rename `token` → `io_token` in the I/O driver callback for clarity
   - Use `push_many()` for local waiters in the cancel-ack drain path
   - Relocate `consume_cancel_ack_locked()` calls into the individual
     task storage match arms (Local/Remote) where the RuntimeState lock
     is already held, eliminating a redundant separate lock acquisition

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
